concerns.text: FR-005 requires a control test where the stress label is shuffled to verify the model isn't just predicting the label. Task T021b implements this correctly. However, SC-002 defines the metric as the 'drop in R² score when training on one stress type and testing on a different stress type'. Task T020b ('Stress-Blind Baseline') regresses out stress effects, which does not measure cross-stress generalization (train on A, test on B). There is no task explicitly implementing the 'train on Drought, test on Salinity' cross-stress evaluation required by SC-002 and FR-005. The plan conflates baseline controls with the cross-stress metric.

Task T020c calculates 'Drop = R²(Within-Stress) - R²(Cross-Stress)'. However, no preceding task (T019-T020b) explicitly implements the 'Cross-Stress' model training and evaluation (training on one stress, testing on another) required to generate the R²(Cross-Stress) value. The task assumes the existence of a cross-stress evaluation result that is not produced by any listed task.

Task T020a trains 'Raw Feature Baseline' models. The plan (plan.md) and spec (SC-002) discuss a 'Raw Feature Baseline' as a control for general proteome-expression links, but the specific metric in SC-002 is the 'drop in R²' between within-stress and cross-stress performance. T020a does not address the cross-stress evaluation gap. A task is missing to explicitly perform the cross-stress split and evaluation (Train on Stress A, Test on Stress B).

Task T013 introduces a 'Fallback: mygene (Python)' logic. While the spec (FR-003) mandates 'biomaRt R package (version 2023-10)', the task adds a fallback. This is a scope deviation from the strict spec requirement for a single deterministic source (Constitution I). While pragmatic, it creates a non-deterministic path not authorized by the spec. A task to explicitly handle the 'biomaRt failure' scenario as a hard stop or to document the deviation is missing.

Task T012 specifies a 'strict 4-step Detection-Limit (DL) hierarchy' (Reported -> Percentile -> Small Sample -> All Missing). FR-002 requires 'Left-Censored Missing (LCM) imputation' but does not authorize this specific custom hierarchy or the fallback logic of dropping columns. The implementation logic is not fully traceable to the spec's requirements without assuming the hierarchy is the only valid interpretation.
